message:
fix(window): NTH_VALUE, CUME_DIST, PERCENT_RANK evaluation + RANGE peer-group semantics

Five window function evaluation fixes in execute_window_select:

1. NTH_VALUE with UNBOUNDED frame incorrectly routed through inverse-counter
   path, returning different values per row instead of constant nth value.
2. CUME_DIST peer-group evaluation had off-by-one from step/inverse/value
   protocol mismatch. Replaced with direct computation.
3. PERCENT_RANK peer-group evaluation called inverse before value, shifting
   cursor past current group. Replaced with direct computation.
4-5. Running SUM/COUNT with ORDER BY (default RANGE frame) treated peers
   independently (ROWS semantics). Added RANGE peer-group evaluation for
   aggregate window functions while excluding numbering functions
   (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG) from peer-grouping.

Added 23 new differential parity tests (14 extended + 9 edge cases).
All 17/17 parity matrix tests pass, 26/26 window tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
